title: Time-bound containers (Milestone, Iteration) — semantics, close triggers, rollforward
description: Formal shape of time-containers.yaml — the two time-bound container types the methodology recognises (Milestone: mandatory primary; Iteration: optional secondary). Per-container fields fix the substrate, whether the container is required on every issue, the typical duration, the close-trigger vocabulary the methodology accepts (`date-based`, `content-based`, `either`), the marker pattern + severity for the trigger declaration, the rollforward cascade behaviour for date-based closes, and the pre-close triage cadence. Top-level cross-cuts: `iteration_usage_patterns` (the three patterns from MET-015) and `cascade_interaction` (how rollforward interacts with the workflow.yaml closure cascade). Consumed by the create-issue / transition-state skills for Milestone-aware filing and the pre-close triage routine. Companion per COR-018.

containers.description: Container entries keyed by container id (`milestone`, `iteration`).

description.description: How Milestone and Iteration are used together — coarser/finer, sprint, themed cluster.

iteration_usage_patterns.description: Three patterns for using Iteration alongside Milestone (or instead of it as a secondary tier). The methodology doesn't impose a pattern; describes how the substrate composes when both layers are in play.

description.description: How rollforward interacts with workflow.yaml's closure cascade.

rolled_forward_descendants_skip_closure_.description: Whether descendants reassigned via rollforward count toward the parent's closure-cascade eligibility. False — only closed children matter for that cascade direction.

substrate.description: Where the container lives in GitHub — `native GitHub Milestone`, `Projects v2 Iteration field`.

required.description: Whether the methodology mandates this container's use. Milestone: true. Iteration: false.

typical_duration.description: Prose describing the typical duration — `project-specific (sprint, release wave, quarter)`.

structural_facts.description: Structural rules that hold regardless of semantics (e.g., `every Milestone has a name, optional due date, close condition`; `an issue is assigned to at most one Milestone`).

title_format_ref.description: Typed-token reference into titles.yaml for the container's title format (Milestone points at the `milestone` entry there). Optional; absent for containers without a title surface (Iteration values are typically short numeric tokens, not formatted titles).

close_triggers.description: Allowed close-trigger values declared on the container itself. Each entry carries the value, behaviour, and rollforward implication.

rollforward_behaviour.description: Mechanics of the rollforward cascade when a date-based container closes with open children. Optional for containers that don't roll forward.

pre_close_triage.description: When and how the agent surfaces an open-children triage prompt before a date-based close.

rollforward.description: Whether open children roll forward when this trigger fires. `date-based`: true; `content-based`: false (by definition no open children); `either`: rollforward only when the date trigger fired.

pattern.description: Regex the container's description's first line must match — `^Close trigger: (date-based|content-based|either)$`.

position.description: Where the marker sits — first line of the container's description, blank line after, then content.

new_filing_severity.description: Severity when a new container is filed without the marker or with an invalid value (hard-reject per MET-013).

inherited_without_marker_severity.description: Severity when an existing container is read without the marker (warning — infer from due-date presence and prompt to write the marker on the next interaction).

fallback_inference.description: How the agent infers the close trigger when the marker is absent on an inherited container. Has due date → `date-based`; no due date → `content-based`.

pattern.description: Regex for the optional `Rollforward target: <Milestone>` line.

wrong_target_severity.description: Severity when the rollforward target references a non-existent or closed container.

steps.description: Ordered actions the agent performs on date-based close.

parent_follow_rule.description: How parents (Features, Umbrellas, EPICs) follow their children across the rollforward — moves with open ones; closed ones stay tagged historically.

lead_time_days_default.description: How many days before due date the agent surfaces the triage prompt. Project-configurable; default 3.

is_gate.description: Whether triage is a gate (blocks close) or a courtesy (informational). False — courtesy.
